arts.ttl: Juventus vs PSV Eindhoven: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Thiago Motta's reign as Juventus coach got off to a bright start with successive 3-0 Serie A victories, but back-to-back goalless draws since show that there's still plenty of work to be done in Turin. Heading into the Champions League off the back claiming a record number of points in an Eredivisie season, PSV have been handed a challenging start to their journey in this year's expanded competition.

arts.ttl: Adrien Rabiot is closing in on a free transfer to Marseille 'subject to the results of a medical' from Juventus - as Lyon boss jokes he would 'prefer it to happen a week later' after their clash
arts.sum: Rabiot could make his debut when Marseille, who sit second in the table, travel to rivals Lyon on Sunday night and Pierre Sage joked the timing of the move could have been better.

arts.ttl: Chiellini expresses excitement over return to Juventus in new executive role
arts.sum: Giorgio Chiellini, the former Juventus captain and Italian football legend, has officially returned to the club in a new capacity. The 39-year-old has been appointed as the Head of Football Institutional Relations, marking a significant transition from his playing career to a management role. Juventus announced the news with an official statement, highlighting Chiellini’s enduring connection to the club: “Giorgio Chiellini has never stopped being a Bianconero: in his heart, in his soul, in the values he has always embodied, both on and off the field. From September 16, he will wear the jersey of a Juventus executive in the The post Chiellini expresses excitement over return to Juventus in new executive role appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Locatelli: ‘Since Thiago Motta arrived, I realised I had to change something’
arts.sum: Juventus midfielder Manuel Locatelli addressed the media ahead of the club’s Champions League opener against PSV Eindhoven. The Italian international shared his thoughts on the team’s return to Europe’s elite competition and his personal growth under new manager Thiago Motta. Locatelli expressed the team’s excitement for their Champions League return, stating, “We missed it last year. It’s the most important competition for clubs. We’re excited and can’t wait to take the field. Hearing the anthem is special, and we want to do it in front of our fans who will support us.” Reflecting on his evolving role within the team, The post Locatelli: ‘Since Thiago Motta arrived, I realised I had to change something’ appeared first on FootItalia.com.
